description: BRCA1 c.5266dupC — the Ashkenazi-founder frameshift duplication classically called '5382insC' in old clinical-lab nomenclature. The duplicated C sits in a CCCC stretch, so the same biological variant has multiple syntactically-valid HGVS forms; HGVS 3'-shift rules select the canonical 3'-most position. This fixture demonstrates the right-shift in action: input c.5266dupC normalizes to genomic NC_000017.11:g.43057065dup (the rightmost C duplication on the reference) and coding NM_007294.4:c.5266dup (note the trailing 'C' is dropped in canonical form since the base is implicit for a single-base dup). Also exercises the heavy-transcript-fan-out case BRCA1 is known for (200+ annotated isoforms).
trimming_note: Captured verbatim, no trimming. ~25 KB; the size IS the test signal — verifies our max_transcript_forms cap works against a real heavy-fan-out response.
